heading: Langwarrin South vs Lardner — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Langwarrin South, Victoria and Lardner, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Langwarrin South has a lower overall climate risk score (41/100) compared to Lardner (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Langwarrin South or Lardner safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Langwarrin South has a flood risk score of 59/100, while Lardner scores 56/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Langwarrin South scores 45/100 for bushfire risk. Lardner scores 41/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Langwarrin South or Lardner?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Langwarrin South has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.78% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
